name: @rntme/identity-auth0
limitations: Only IntrospectSession is claimed via OIDC JWKS validation. RevokeSession, ListSessions, GetSession remain unclaimed because Auth0 server-side sessions do not map to the canonical Session entity (no listable session resource, no programmatic revoke without Mgmt session-scope).
GetMembership is not claimed because Auth0 organization membership reads are naturally scoped by organization and member rather than a stable standalone membership resource.

UpdateMembership is not claimed because Auth0 role assignment APIs add and remove role ids separately; a canonical replacement-style role update would risk leaving stale roles without a read/compare/delete cycle.
Membership roles use Auth0 role ids; role name and permission expansion is not guaranteed by this module.
Auth0 organization invitations are organization-scoped; listing or retrieving invitations requires the organization id in adapter inputs or metadata.
Auth0 exposes app_metadata and user_metadata differently from canonical public/private/unsafe metadata; mappings preserve visibility best-effort and keep vendor_raw for audit.
Lifecycle events are translated from Auth0 log/event payloads when the log record contains enough identity, organization, or invitation data.
Auth0 Management API mutations do not accept rntme idempotency keys or correlation ids; callers must use rntme-side retry/dedupe controls for command replay safety.
